interactions.final_answer:
## PHASE 0 — DESIGN PROPOSAL (IKEA) ### 1) SCOPE CONFIG **Scope name:** IKEA Workplace (Democratic Design Hub) **Scope description:**

A shared intranet for IKEA co-workers to stay aligned on priorities across both ownership groups—practical updates, policies, and the way we work.

**Welcome message:**

Welcome, co-worker. Here you’ll find the information you need to do your job well—without the noise: leadership updates, practical guidance, and policies that keep us safe and consistent.

If something changes, you’ll see it here, in simple language.

**Scope hero image (note for Phase 1.5):** Logo upload URL from your profile (logo.dev): https://img.logo.dev/ikea.com?token=pk_OwST1E04QH-w3oM0YksM2Q&retina=true **Brand colors (Diggspace mapping):** - Primary color: **#0058AB** (primary_color_contrast: **#FFFFFF**) - Secondary color: **#FBD914** - Warn color: **#E00751** (warn_color_contrast: **#FFFFFF**) **Highlights settings:** - number_of_highlights: **4** - highlights_carousel: **true** - highlights_style: **BannerCarousel** **Footer (3 columns with real links from profile):** 1. **About IKEA / Careers / Investors** - About: https://about.ikea.com - Careers: https://www.ikea.com/global/en/this-is-ikea/work-with-us/ - Investors/financial: https://www.ingka.com/annual-summary-and-sustainability-report/ 2. **Newsroom** - Ingka Newsroom: https://www.ingka.com/newsroom/ - Inter IKEA Newsroom: https://www.inter.ikea.com/en/newsroom/ 3. **Support & Sustainability** - Sustainability hub: https://www.ikea.com/global/en/our-business/sustainability/ - IKEA Foundation: https://www.ikeafoundation.org **Social links (real from profile):** - LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/ikea/ - YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/ikea - X/Twitter: https://twitter.com/IKEA - Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/ikea/ **navigation_links** - **Topbarlinks (3):** 1. https://www.ingka.com/newsroom/ 2. https://www.inter.ikea.com/en/newsroom/ 3. https://www.ikea.com/global/en/our-business/sustainability/ - **footerLinks (5):** 1. https://about.ikea.com 2. https://www.ikea.com/global/en/this-is-ikea/work-with-us/ 3. https://www.ingka.com/annual-summary-and-sustainability-report/ 4. https://www.inter.ikea.com/en/performance/ 5. https://www.ikeafoundation.org - **socialLinks:** (LinkedIn, YouTube, X, Instagram as above) **components (pick 2–3 based on profile):** - **upcomingEvents** (event-driven org moments: town halls, store milestones) - **recentDocuments** (document-heavy compliance/policy acknowledgements) - **workAnniversaries** (recognition fits IKEA’s “together” culture) --- ### 2) CHANNEL ARCHITECTURE (5 channels) > Primary channels = pinned (is_sticky=true) and highlights-eligible (hide_highlights=false). > Secondary channels = hide_highlights=true. #### Ch1 (PRIMARY) — “IKEA Inside: Co-worker Updates” - **Purpose:** Operational, factual updates—performance context, launches, store news, and what it means “on the floor.” - **Type:** public - **Color:** **#0058AB** - **is_sticky:** **True** - **image_url:** **stock/financial_report.png** - **FAQ (skip for broadcast):** not needed - **hide_on_homepage_feed:** False - **hide_highlights:** False - **Description:**

For the things that change how we work this week—not just what happened last quarter.

You’ll find clear updates from both ownership structures (Ingka Group and Inter IKEA Group) and the practical “so what” for co-workers.

- **Tabs:** ['Articles'] - **initial_tab:** 'Articles' - **default_role:** 'Reader' #### Ch2 (PRIMARY) — “Leadership Letters & Decisions” - **Purpose:** CEO and leadership communications—decisions, context, and how we’re staying aligned. - **Type:** corporate - **Color:** **#0058AB** - **is_sticky:** **True** - **image_url:** **stock/leadership_speech.png** - **FAQ (2-3 Q&A):** 1. **Q:** Who do we contact if a leadership decision impacts our local processes? **A:** Start with your local People & Operations contact, then escalate through your normal decision-path. If there’s a policy or compliance angle, the acknowledgement section in this hub applies. 2. **Q:** Will letters be translated for local co-workers? **A:** Yes. IKEA localises internal content into 11+ languages, with English as common business language. 3. **Q:** How do we submit questions for town-hall follow-ups? **A:** Use the next Town Hall topic thread inside the Events channel (and your local manager briefing process). - **hide_on_homepage_feed:** False - **hide_highlights:** False - **Description:**

Leadership messages are short, direct, and accountable: what changed, why it matters, and what we do next.

We aim for clarity over buzzwords—because “the IKEA way” expects decisions to be usable, not performative.

- **Tabs:** ['Articles'] - **initial_tab:** 'Articles' - **default_role:** 'None' #### Ch3 (PRIMARY) — “People & Culture” - **Purpose:** HR programs, benefits, onboarding rhythms, and the culture that keeps IKEA flat and effective. - **Type:** public - **Color:** **#FBD914** - **is_sticky:** **True** - **image_url:** **stock/community.png** - **FAQ (2-3 Q&A):** 1. **Q:** What does “co-worker” mean in IKEA internal communications? **A:** It’s a deliberate signal of equality. Internally we use “co-worker,” never “employees” or “staff.” 2. **Q:** How does Tack! work? **A:** Tack! is a pension contribution programme for eligible co-workers—everyone receives the same contribution amount. 3. **Q:** Does IKEA support hybrid work for office roles? **A:** Yes—office-based co-workers operate on hybrid schedules, supported as a retention and wellbeing tool. - **hide_on_homepage_feed:** False - **hide_highlights:** False - **Description:**

Benefits and programs should feel human. Here you’ll find the basics of key IKEA co-worker initiatives—explained simply.

We also share what culture looks like in practice: togetherness, simplicity, and “the IKEA way” of solving problems with fewer steps.

- **Tabs:** ['Articles'] - **initial_tab:** 'Articles' - **default_role:** 'Reader' #### Ch4 (SECONDARY) — “Democratic Design & Circularity” - **Purpose:** Product philosophy updates—Democratic Design in practice, and circular initiatives like second-hand. - **Type:** public - **Color:** **#0071BC** - **is_sticky:** False - **image_url:** **stock/Innovation.png** - **FAQ (2-3 Q&A):** 1. **Q:** What does Democratic Design require from products? **A:** Five dimensions at once: Form, Function, Quality, Sustainability, and Low Price. Affordability is non-negotiable. 2. **Q:** What is the “As-Is” approach in stores? **A:** Many stores include second-hand style sections for value capture and reduced waste; the digital second-hand marketplace formalises that at scale. 3. **Q:** Where is IKEA scaling circular initiatives first? **A:** The second-hand marketplace began in Sweden (Jan 2026) with planned expansion to Spain, Norway, Portugal, and Poland. - **hide_on_homepage_feed:** False - **hide_highlights:** True - **Description:**

Democratic Design is our design engine: we set the price target first, then design around it—so a better everyday life doesn’t stop at aspiration.

We’ll also keep you updated on circularity—because “less waste” is not a side project.

- **Tabs:** ['Articles', 'Pages'] - **initial_tab:** 'Articles' - **default_role:** 'Reader' #### Ch5 (SECONDARY) — “Policy Hub: Safety, Security & Read Me” - **Purpose:** Policy and compliance acknowledgements (security, risk, legal expectations) that require “I have read this.” - **Type:** corporate - **Color:** **#E00751** - **is_sticky:** False - **image_url:** **stock/security.png** - **FAQ (2-3 Q&A):** 1. **Q:** Why do some policies require confirmation? **A:** For compliance and safety. We need co-workers to explicitly acknowledge key instructions. 2. **Q:** What changes if I’m unsure how a policy applies to my role? **A:** Ask through your People & Operations contact first. If it’s security-related, follow the guidance and escalation path in the policy. 3. **Q:** Are policies translated? **A:** Yes—content is localised into 11+ languages where required. - **hide_on_homepage_feed:** True - **hide_highlights:** True - **Description:**

This channel collects policies, security guidance, and compliance expectations in one place.

When a policy requires read confirmation, please complete it—simple and direct.

- **Tabs:** ['Articles', 'Pages'] - **initial_tab:** 'Articles' - **default_role:** 'None' --- ### 3) CONTENT CALENDAR (live, populated) **Hub highlights (exactly 4 items total):** - 1) Ch1 — “FY25 at a glance: price reductions, profit recovery, and what changed” - 2) Ch2 — “Leadership transitions: what Juvencio Maeztu and Jakub Jankowski are prioritising” - 3) Ch5 — “Cyber security essentials for co-workers: mandatory acknowledgement (2026)” - 4) Ch4 — “Second-hand at scale: Sweden launches a digital marketplace (Jan 2026)” --- #### Ch1 (PRIMARY) — IKEA Inside: Co-worker Updates (3 items) 1) **Title:** FY25 at a glance: price reductions, profit recovery, and what changed - **Type:** recap - **tags:** ['fy25', 'prices', 'financials', 'ingka'] - **is_main_highlight:** **True** - **is_sticky:** **True** - **require_read_confirmation:** False - **status:** 'Published' - **notify:** **True** - **image_url:** stock/financial_report.png - **Body outline:**

Quarter context, revenue/profit movement, and the “side with the many people” rationale explained for co-workers.

2) **Title:** Oxford Street opened in May 2025: a city-format store prototype to watch - **Type:** spotlight - **tags:** ['store-opening', 'urban-strategy', 'operations'] - **is_main_highlight:** False - **is_sticky:** True - **require_read_confirmation:** False - **status:** 'Published' - **notify:** False - **image_url:** stock/office.png - **Body outline:**

What makes the 82,000 sq ft Oxford Street format different (electric heating, no car park, As-Is) and how we learn from it.

3) **Title:** New store formats: up to 20 small-format openings planned by Sept 2026 - **Type:** announcement - **tags:** ['expansion', 'north-america', 'smaller-city', 'hiring'] - **is_main_highlight:** False - **is_sticky:** False - **require_read_confirmation:** False - **status:** 'Published' - **notify:** **True** - **image_url:** stock/warehouse.png - **Body outline:**

Why faster-to-open formats matter, the co-worker roles attached (~500), and what to expect operationally.

--- #### Ch2 (PRIMARY) — Leadership Letters & Decisions (3 items) 1) **Title:** Leadership transitions: what Juvencio Maeztu and Jakub Jankowski are prioritising - **Type:** announcement - **tags:** ['leadership', 'strategy', 'culture', '2026'] - **is_main_highlight:** **True** - **is_sticky:** True - **require_read_confirmation:** False - **status:** 'Published' - **notify:** **True** - **image_url:** stock/leadership_speech.png - **Body outline:**

Summarise the dual CEO changes (Nov 2025 / Jan 2026), framing around faster decisions and affordability, in plain language.

2) **Title:** Leaner support functions: 800 roles in Ingka Group and why decision speed matters - **Type:** policy - **tags:** ['restructuring', 'group-functions', 'sweden', 'decision-making'] - **is_main_highlight:** False - **is_sticky:** True - **require_read_confirmation:** **True** - **status:** 'Published' - **notify:** **True** - **image_url:** stock/risk.png - **Body outline:**

Explain March 2026 elimination (~800 roles, 625 in Sweden), what it targets, and what co-workers should do if their workflows are affected.

3) **Title:** Inter IKEA FY25 context: tariff pressures and operating profit down 26% - **Type:** recap - **tags:** ['inter-ikea', 'tariffs', 'fy25', 'profit'] - **is_main_highlight:** False - **is_sticky:** False - **require_read_confirmation:** False - **status:** 'Published' - **notify:** False - **image_url:** stock/stock_market.png - **Body outline:**

Operating profit €1.7B (-26%), net income ~-32%, and the “why” behind absorbed price reductions and sourcing costs.

--- #### Ch3 (PRIMARY) — People & Culture (3 items) 1) **Title:** Tack! and solid benefits: what stays the same—even as we reorganise - **Type:** guide - **tags:** ['benefits', 'tack!', 'pensions', 'co-workers'] - **is_main_highlight:** False - **is_sticky:** True - **require_read_confirmation:** False - **status:** 'Published' - **notify:** False - **image_url:** stock/career.png - **Body outline:**

Explain Tack! contribution principle (equal contribution), loyalty leave logic, and co-worker discount—simple and actionable.

2) **Title:** Learning & growth at IKEA: digital learning plus onboarding that mixes practice and class - **Type:** guide - **tags:** ['learning', 'onboarding', 'development', 'leadership'] - **is_main_highlight:** False - **is_sticky:** False - **require_read_confirmation:** False - **status:** 'Published' - **notify:** False - **image_url:** stock/online_learning.png - **Body outline:**

What new co-workers should expect, how leadership programs and mobility work, and how “the IKEA way” shows up in training.

3) **Title:** Hybrid reality for office roles: how we use it to stay well and stay effective - **Type:** spotlight - **tags:** ['hybrid-work', 'wellbeing', 'retention', 'office'] - **is_main_highlight:** False - **is_sticky:** False - **require_read_confirmation:** False - **status:** 'Published' - **notify:** False - **image_url:** stock/team_diversity.png - **Body outline:**

Why hybrid exists as a wellbeing tool, and what “simple schedules” look like when priorities shift.

--- #### Ch4 (SECONDARY) — Democratic Design & Circularity (2 items) 1) **Title:** Second-hand at scale: Sweden launches a digital marketplace (Jan 2026) - **Type:** milestone - **tags:** ['circularity', 'second-hand', 'sweden', 'digital'] - **is_main_highlight:** **True** - **is_sticky:** True - **require_read_confirmation:** False - **status:** 'Published' - **notify:** **True** - **image_url:** stock/sustainability.png - **Body outline:**

What launched in Sweden, why IKEA captures secondary value, and which countries are planned next.

2) **Title:** Renewable energy progress: FY25 matched 94.8% of operations with renewables - **Type:** recap - **tags:** ['renewables', 'sustainability', 'fy25', 'energy'] - **is_main_highlight:** False - **is_sticky:** False - **require_read_confirmation:** False - **status:** 'Published' - **notify:** False - **image_url:** stock/sustainability.png - **Body outline:**

94.8% matched vs goal of 100% by 2025, €4.2B invested/committed, and €3.3B planned through 2030.

--- #### Ch5 (SECONDARY) — Policy Hub: Safety, Security & Read Me (2 items) 1) **Title:** Cyber security essentials for co-workers: mandatory acknowledgement (2026) - **Type:** policy - **tags:** ['security', 'passwords', 'risk', 'acknowledgement'] - **is_main_highlight:** **True** - **is_sticky:** True - **require_read_confirmation:** **True** - **status:** 'Published' - **notify:** **True** - **image_url:** stock/Password.png - **Body outline:**

Practical rules around passwords, safe access, and reporting suspicious activity—ending with “acknowledge here.”

2) **Title:** Data handling & document review: minimum expectations for everyday work - **Type:** policy - **tags:** ['data', 'document_review', 'quality_check', 'compliance'] - **is_main_highlight:** False - **is_sticky:** False - **require_read_confirmation:** **True** - **status:** 'Published' - **notify:** False - **image_url:** stock/document_review.png - **Body outline:**

How we handle data collections/maintenance with quality checks (clear, repeatable steps) and what to do when unsure.

--- ### 2 small events across the hub (exactly 2 total events) #### Event A (Ch2 corporate is allowed for events) **Channel:** Ch2 — Leadership Letters & Decisions - **Title:** Town hall with leaders: open questions on decision speed and affordability - **Type:** event - **tags:** ['town-hall', 'leadership', 'q-a', '2026'] - **is_main_highlight:** False - **is_sticky:** False - **require_read_confirmation:** False - **status:** 'Published' - **notify:** False - **image_url:** stock/video_call.png - **event_start/event_end:** **2026-06-20T10:00:00Z** → **2026-06-20T11:00:00Z** - **location:** Online + local manager briefing rooms - **Body outline:**

What the session covers, how questions will be collected, and how follow-ups map back to co-worker actions.

#### Event B (Ch1 public) **Channel:** Ch1 — IKEA Inside: Co-worker Updates - **Title:** Store format learning day: Oxford Street + city prototypes (what we’re testing now) - **Type:** event - **tags:** ['store-format', 'learning', 'operations', 'urban-strategy'] - **is_main_highlight:** False - **is_sticky:** True - **require_read_confirmation:** False - **status:** 'Published' - **notify:** True - **image_url:** stock/office.png - **event_start/event_end:** **2026-06-27T13:00:00Z** → **2026-06-27T14:30:00Z** - **location:** Älmhult + regional hubs (hybrid) - **Body outline:**

A practical agenda: what’s working in electric heating layouts, As-Is learnings, and how we share improvements fast.

--- ### 4) HUB-LEVEL POLISH (global settings) - **company_name:** IKEA - **website_name:** IKEA - **default_language:** **en-US** (inferred: global corporate context in profile uses English + HQ Netherlands/Sweden; if you want another, tell me which) - **supported_languages:** Since the profile states “English + 11+ languages” but doesn’t list exact tags, I can’t enumerate exact language codes without guessing. - **Question needed:** Which language tags should we set (e.g. ['en-US','nl-NL','sv-SE','pl-PL']), or should I keep it empty/unchanged? - **login_quick_links (5 real URLs from profile):** 1. https://www.ikea.com/global/en/this-is-ikea/work-with-us/ 2. https://www.ingka.com/newsroom/ 3. https://www.inter.ikea.com/en/newsroom/ 4. https://www.ikea.com/global/en/our-business/sustainability/ 5. https://about.ikea.com - **sidebar_navigation (priority order):** 1. **collaborators** (IKEA is people-first, flat culture, togetherness) 2. **documents** (policies/policy acknowledgements) 3. **learning** (onboarding + digital learning) 4. **apps** (tools/ways of working) 5. **workgroups** (local/store collaboration needs) 6. **approvalRequests** (policy/compliance workflows readiness) 7. **ideation** (bottom-up “Let’s Co-Report” spirit) --- ## APPROVAL CHECK (IMPORTANT) Before I can build, I need one small clarification: 1) **Global settings: supported_languages** Your profile says “translated into 11+ languages” but doesn’t provide the exact language tags. **What exact list of language tags should we set?** (Example: `["en-US","nl-NL","sv-SE","pl-PL"]`) If you don’t care, tell me “leave supported_languages unchanged”. 2) Confirm the **default_language**: should it be **en-US**, or do you want a different default? --- **This is a complete design. Shall I build it end-to-end, or would you like to adjust anything first — channel names, content plan, colors, hub settings (including supported languages/default language)?**
